description: Takes alignment files in SAM/BAM format and a feature file in GTF/GFF format (usually with exon annotation), name sorts the alignment file and calculates for each sample the number of reads mapping to each feature. (HTSeq.count v0.11.2)
author: Barbara Hill;Broad Institute, Guy Bottu;VIB BioinformaticsCore
plan:
## HTSeq.Count GenePattern Module — Implementation Plan

### 1. Overview
HTSeq.Count is a GenePattern module that Takes alignment files in SAM/BAM format and a feature file in GTF/GFF format (usually with exon annotation), name sorts the alignment file and calculates for each sample the number of reads mapping to each feature. (HTSeq.count v0.11.2)

### 2. Parameters
The module exposes 14 parameters:
- Required: input.files, GTF.file, strandedness, min.qual, mode, count.nonunique, count.secondary, count.supplementary, feature.type, id.type, output.file, output.format
- Optional: sample.names, gene.name

### 3. Command Line
```
/htseq_count/htseqcount_input_detect_and_sort.py --htseqcount htseq-count --input <input.files> <sample.names> --gtf <GTF.file> --strand <strandedness> --minqual <min.qual> --mode <mode> --nonunique <count.nonunique> --secondary <count.secondary> --supplementary <count.supplementary> --featuretype <feature.type> --idtype <id.type> <gene.name> --output <output.file> --outformat <output.format>
```

### 4. Docker Image
Docker image: genepattern/htseq_count\:0.6

### 5. Validation Strategy
- Validate all required parameters are provided
- Validate file formats match expected extensions
- Check parameter value ranges where applicable

### 6. Implementation Roadmap
Phase 1: Wrapper script implementation
Phase 2: Docker container setup
Phase 3: Parameter validation and error handling
Phase 4: Testing and documentation

parameters.name: input.files
parameters.description: Input file(s) in SAM or BAM format.

parameters.name: sample.names
parameters.description: Text file with the names of the samples, one per line (optional and only relevant if you request Excel or GCT format). The names in the file must be in the same order as the input SAM/BAM files. If you do not provide a file the sample names will be derived from the SAM/BAM file names.

parameters.name: GTF.file
parameters.description: A GTF or GFF file containing a list of gene model annotations. (This file can be gzipped)

parameters.name: strandedness
parameters.description:
none \: a read is considered overlapping with a feature regardless of whether it is mapped to the same or the opposite strand as the feature.

forward \: a single-end read has to be mapped to the same strand as the feature, for paired-end reads the first read has to be on the same strand and the second read on the opposite strand.

reverse \: the above rules are reversed.

set to 'none' if your input file contains no strand information.

parameters.name: min.qual
parameters.description: Minimum quality to accept a read.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Mode to handle reads overlapping more than one feature. Consult the documentation for a full explanation.

parameters.name: count.nonunique
parameters.description: Whether to count reads that are not uniquely aligned or are ambiguously assigned to features.

parameters.name: count.secondary
parameters.description: Whether to count secondary alignments (which are marked in the SAM/BAM file by a 0x100 flag).

parameters.name: count.supplementary
parameters.description: Whether to count supplementary alignments (which are marked in the SAM/BAM file by a 0x800 flag).

parameters.name: feature.type
parameters.description: Name in the 3<sup>rd</sup> column of the GTF/GFF input file that is used to identify the features that must be counted.

parameters.name: id.type
parameters.description: GTF/GFF attribute used to group features.

parameters.name: gene.name
parameters.description: GTF/GFF attribute with the name of the gene or some other information that can help to identify the gene in a more user-friendly way than the ID (optional). If you fill this in an extra column will be added to the output table. For Ensembl data gene_id is suitable.

parameters.name: output.file
parameters.description: output file name - Inputfile_Basename.Output_Filename (if you choose GCT format .gct will automatically be added)

choices.display: with header added, suitable for import in Excel
